examples: Gandhi was accused by some of trying to appease both factions of the electorate.
Children aren't born knowing how to say "I'm sorry"; rather, they learn over time that such statements appease parents and friends and their own consciences.
Or is it simply a move to appease its critics?
To appease the pixies, Guernsey folk would leave a bowl of porridge out at night.
Everyone did, in fact, but that likely didn't appease many critics of GoDaddy's lad-mag-style commercials.
